lever: replace the naive O(V*E) all-edges-every-pass Bellman-Ford (which re-walked the slow G.edges(data=True) EdgeView each pass AND picked different predecessors than nx on ties) with a faithful in-process port of networkx's scan-based Goldberg-Radzik algorithm, operating on a one-time native to_dict_of_dicts adjacency snapshot ({u:{v:w}}). Fixes BOTH the perf gap and a latent pred-divergence correctness bug.
correctness_fix: fnx's old directed goldberg_radzik returned a DIFFERENT predecessor dict than nx on tie-heavy graphs (naive BF tie-break != nx Goldberg-Radzik). The conformance fixtures had no ties so it passed; tie-heavy graphs diverged. The port matches nx's exact (pred,dist).

parity.coverage: weighted, negative weights, negative-cycle detection, tie-heavy gnp; fair rebuild-from-edges baseline

headline: goldberg_radzik directed: ~2.3x self-speedup (naive BF -> nx scan algo), gap 4.21x slower -> ~1.2x near-parity, AND fixes a latent predecessor-divergence correctness bug (now byte-exact to nx). Pure Python, no rebuild.
residual: ~1.2-1.4x vs nx = to_dict_of_dicts snapshot + plain-Python algo vs nx native _adj; near-parity.
